title: pop()
subtitle: Remove from the end of an array
goal: Learn to remove the last element of an array using pop().
explanation: pop() removes the last element of an array.
After pop() is called, the array becomes shorter.
The method changes the original array.
It specifically removes the last element.
pop() is often used when working with a stack of data.

analogy.title: Analogy
analogy.text: Imagine a stack of plates. pop() removes the top plate from the stack.

remember: It works with the end of an array.

mistake.title: Common mistake
mistake.text: Beginners sometimes think pop() removes the first element. In fact, it only works with the last one.

quiz.question: What remains after calling pop() on ["Apple", "Banana", "Orange"]?
